pi_items.source: MITRE ATT&CK (G0034 for Sandworm, G0032 for Lazarus Group); Wikipedia; official threat intelligence reports
export_result.sh lines 65-74: GOV_VISITS variable checks for 6 government domains, not just cisa.gov
README.md lines 24-26; export_result.sh line 79 confirms case-insensitive match for '%apt research%'
README.md line 26 states: 'Sub-folder "Lazarus Group" (or "Lazarus")'; export_result.sh line 97 accepts '%lazarus%' OR '%apt38%' OR '%hidden cobra%'
README.md line 34; export_result.sh line 135 confirms exact path
README.md lines 25-26; verifier.py line 106-131 checks for ≥3 bookmarks per sub-folder
README.md line 6 states singular form: '**Occupation**: Intelligence Analyst'

privileged_info_summary: This is a cybersecurity OSINT task requiring research on two nation-state APT groups: Sandworm (Russian GRU Unit 74455, also known as APT44/Voodoo Bear) and Lazarus Group (North Korean RGB, also known as APT38/Hidden Cobra). Sandworm is confirmed responsible for NotPetya, Ukraine power grid attacks, and Olympic Destroyer malware. Lazarus Group is confirmed responsible for the Sony Pictures hack, WannaCry ransomware, Bangladesh Bank heist ($81 million, 2016), and Ronin Bridge hack ($625 million, 2022). The task requires creating Firefox bookmarks in an 'APT Research' folder with minimum 3 sources per group, visiting MITRE ATT&CK and government advisory sites, and producing a threat intelligence report at ~/Desktop/threat_intel_report.txt containing group names, MITRE T-codes (T-format attack techniques), and nation-state attribution.
